content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

One correctness issue was found in the PID liveness implementation; otherwise no additional blocking findings in reviewed files.

## Amendments

1. Fix `FileSystem::proc_start_ticks` to read the correct `/proc/<pid>/stat` field for process start-time validation. It currently reads `fields.get(19)` (the obsolete `itrealvalue` field) instead of the `starttime` field (`fields.get(20)` after the `) ` split). This makes PID-reuse detection in `is_pid_alive` ineffective (often always comparing `0`), so stale-running snapshots can be treated as alive in `run status`, `run resume` reconciliation, and `run stop` prechecks, defeating the new lifecycle recovery guarantees.
   Rationale: Robust stale-process detection depends on reliable PID identity. Reading the wrong stat field is a correctness gap that can prevent stale runs from being recovered and allows stop/resume logic to miss dead orchestrators.
